channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Officials demand answers as crews work to restore power after another Puerto Rico blackout
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Crews worked early Thursday to restore power to Puerto Rico after a blackout across the entire island that affected the main international airport, several hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers The outage that began past noon Wednesday left 1.4 million cust
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE targets a community service program in its latest cost-cutting effort
item.description: A 30-year-old community service program for young adults that operates across the U.S. has become the latest target of the Trump administration’s campaign to slash government spending.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Fingerhut, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Arson attack probe at Pennsylvania governor's mansion looking into suspect's hatred of Josh Shapiro
item.description: HERSHEY, Pa. (AP) — Authorities investigating why a man set fire to Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s official residence are probing whether the suspect was motivated by the Democrat's Jewish faith or positions on Israel’s war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Temu and Shein say they're raising prices due to tariffs
item.description: Both companies said they would be making “price adjustments” starting April 25, although neither provided details about the size of the increases.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: AmeriCorps members who respond to disasters and help nonprofits are let go in DOGE cuts
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Young volunteers who respond to natural disasters and help with community projects across the U.S. have been discharged as a result of the Trump administration 's campaign to shrink government workforce and services.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Proposed rule change on endangered species triggers alarm for environmentalists
item.description: The Trump administration plans to rewrite part of the Endangered Species Act that prohibits harming the habitats of endangered and threatened species.
item.dc:creator: Tammy Webber, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lawyers file wrongful death notice against Idaho city for family of boy shot by police
item.description: A California law firm notified the city of Pocatello, Idaho, on Wednesday of its intention to file a federal wrongful death lawsuit on the behalf of the family of Victor Perez, a 17-year-old boy with intellectual disabilities who was shot multiple times by four police officers.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3 dead in multi-boat crash during Major League Fishing tournament in Alabama
item.description: A fatal boat crash during a Major League Fishing tournament in Alabama is currently under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Austin Franklin, ABC 4

item.title: Texas DOGE calls for sanctions, audit of insurance company accused of spying
item.description: DOGE Lawmakers and Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton have accused Superior of hiring private investigators to get information on lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Dylan McKim, NewsNation

item.title: California sues to stop Trump from imposing sweeping tariffs
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California Gov. Gavin Newsom sued the Trump administration on Wednesday, challenging the president's authority to impose sweeping tariffs that have set off a global trade war.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: MLB ump leaves game after foul ball hits him in the face
item.description: Veteran umpire Hunter Wendelstedt instantly hit the ground after he took a direct hit from the line smash off the bat of New York Mets center fielder Tyrone Taylor in the seventh inning.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Family says ICE agents smashed car window in seizing Guatemalan man who's seeking asylum
item.description: A Massachusetts family is demanding answers from U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, complaining its agents smashed a car window and detained a man who was in the process of applying for asylum.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey And Rodrique Ngowi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Walgreens class action drug settlement: Thursday deadline for $100M
item.description: The lawsuit alleges Walgreens sold generic drugs at a higher cost to customers with insurance than to Prescription Savings Club members, according to court docs.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Zoom service restored after outage affects thousands
item.description: Thousands of Zoom users reported issues accessing virtual meetings Wednesday, with the site confirming shortly after noon it was investigating the issue.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Van Hollen denied meeting, phone call with Abrego Garcia during El Salvador visit
item.description: The Salvadoran government on Wednesday rebuffed Sen. Chris Van Hollen’s (D-Md.) request to meet or speak with a wrongfully deported Maryland man, the senator said, accusing the Trump administration and others of lying about Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s gang ties.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McDonald's teases the return of the Snack Wrap
item.description: McDonald's has finally teased a re-release date for a once-popular menu item. Kind of. In a relatively cryptic post to X on Tuesday, the fast-food chain announced the beloved Snack Wraps would return on the 14th.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, KTLA 5

item.title: 2 service members dead after vehicle crashes near NM Border Patrol station
item.description: Two service members were killed and a third seriously injured in a crash on Tuesday morning in Santa Teresa, New Mexico, according to U.S. Northern Command on behalf of Joint Task Force-Southern Border.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Luna, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Starbucks introducing new dress code for baristas, a move protested by some
item.description: Baristas at your local Starbucks will soon be sporting a new, more consistent look after the company announced a new dress code this week.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KRON4

item.title: New Jersey Transit train engineers reject labor deal with management
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — New Jersey Transit train engineers have rejected a labor agreement with management, raising the potential for a strike or a lockout next month.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Maryland senator to visit El Salvador to check on mistakenly deported man
item.description: Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D) said he is traveling to El Salvador on Wednesday to check on the status of a deported legal resident.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gambian ex-soldier convicted at US trial of torturing suspected backers of a failed 2006 coup
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former member of Gambia's military was convicted in federal court Tuesday of torturing five people accused of involvement in a failed coup against the West African country's longtime dictator nearly 20 years ago, capping a rare prosecution in the United States for torture committed a
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man charged with arson at Pennsylvania governor's home struggled with mental health, brother says
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A man accused of setting fire to Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s mansion over the weekend had struggled with mental illness, twice being treated at a psychiatric hospital, as his life unraveled dramatically in the past few years, his brother said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Video shows ICE agents shatter car window during Massachusetts arrest: attorney
item.description: Federal immigration agents are accused of shattering a car window in Massachusetts while detaining a Guatemalan man on Monday in a caught-on-camera incident – despite his attorney saying they had the wrong person.
item.dc:creator: Jusolyn Flower, KRON4

item.title: NTSB finds 'failure' in training before deadly Newark cargo ship fire
item.description: The Newark fire department's lack of training and familiarity with marine firefighting during a deadly cargo ship fire at the East Coast's largest port in July 2023 amounted to a “failure of leadership,” the head of the National Transportation Safety Board said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A look at the universities with federal funding targeted by the Trump administration
item.description: Harvard University is the latest in a growing list of higher education institutions that had its federal funding targeted by the government in order to comply with the Trump administration's political agenda. The series of threats — and subsequent pauses in funding — to some of the top U.S.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man allegedly held captive by stepmother in Connecticut releases first public statement
item.description: In his first public statement, the man allegedly held captive in his home for over 20 years by his stepmother says he's been getting stronger as he begins the process of reclaiming his life.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge rules against parents who wore wristbands to protest transgender student athlete
item.description: A federal judge ruled Monday that two fathers cannot wear pink wristbands with two X’s — the symbol for female chromosomes — drawn on them in opposition to a transgender high school athlete on one of their daughters’ rival soccer teams.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KRON4

item.title: Federal judge drops contempt case against ICE agent over arrest outside Boston courthouse
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge has dismissed a contempt case against an agent for the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement whose arrest of a man during his trial sparked criticism among the law enforcement community in Boston.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, after the institution said it would defy the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Watch: Elephants at San Diego park form protective circle during earthquake
item.description: Watch: Elephants at the San Diego Zoo Safari Park surrounded the younger elephants in a protective "alert circle" when a 5.2 magnitude earthquake struck the region Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, WGN 9

item.title: Video shows mother pick up baby and run during Southern California earthquake
item.description: A quick-thinking San Diego County mother saved her baby from a 5.2 magnitude earthquake in eastern San Diego County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Vance fumbles Ohio State's NCAA football championship trophy as Buckeyes visit the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President JD Vance ended the Ohio State football team's visit to the White House on Monday by fumbling the team's national championship trophy.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: White House launches national security investigation into pharma, semiconductors
item.description: The Trump administration disclosed Monday that it had opened an investigation into the effects on national security of importing certain pharmaceuticals, a move widely seen as the prelude to initiating tariffs on prescription drugs.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: A celestial 'smiley face' will be visible to stargazers later this month: What to know
item.description: Stargazers who turn their eyes skyward — and use a little bit of imagination — will have a chance to spot a rare "smiley face" in space later this month.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, Fox 5

item.title: Boxing legend George Foreman remembered for his love of family, faith and cheeseburgers
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — George Foreman was remembered Monday in a memorial service in his hometown of Houston for his legendary boxing career as well as for his love of God, family, horses and cheeseburgers and for his desire to help his fellow man. “He preached love all the time.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Here's a look at recent aircraft tragedies, mishaps and close calls
item.description: The crash of a small plane in New York over the weekend killed all six on board — a physician couple and their accomplished children and partners — during a family trip to the Catskills.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump, Bukele say they won't return mistakenly deported man to US
item.description: Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele said Monday that he has no plans to return a Maryland man wrongfully deported to a prison in his country, telling reporters, “Of course I’m not going to do it.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9
